---
title: "Longitudinal exploration"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
library(haven)        # Read SPSS files
library(tidyverse)    # Data manipulation and visualization
library(skimr)        # Summary statistics
library(corrplot)     # Correlation plots
library(scales)       # Number formatting
library(ggthemes)     # themes


current_dir <- getwd()
source(file.path(current_dir,"src","R","utils.R"))

# Define years
years <- c(2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2023, 2025)
#years <- c(2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2023 )

# Initialize empty list
waves <- list()

data_path <- "/path/to/data/SHEDS"

# Loop through years
for (year in years) {
  file_path <- paste0(data_path, year, ".sav")
  wave_name <- paste0("w", year)

  cat("Reading", year, "...\n")
  waves[[wave_name]] <- read_clean_sheds(file_path)
  cat(nrow(waves[[wave_name]]), "valid respondents\n")
}

# Find total unique IDs across all waves
all_ids <- waves %>%
  map_df(~ select(.x, id)) %>%  # Change 'id' to your actual ID column name
  pull(id) %>%
  unique()

cat("\nTotal unique IDs across all waves:", length(all_ids), "\n")

cat("\n=== Summary ===\n")
cat("Total respondents (all waves):", sum(map_int(waves, nrow)), "\n")
cat("Total unique IDs:", length(all_ids), "\n")
cat("Duplicate appearances:", sum(map_int(waves, nrow)) - length(all_ids), "\n")

# To see how many times each ID appears
id_counts <- waves %>%
  map_df(~ select(.x, id), .id = "wave") %>%
  count(id) %>%
  arrange(desc(n))

cat("\nDistribution of appearances:\n")
print(table(id_counts$n))
```

Build car history to see EV ownership over time:
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)
library(knitr)

# Convert waves list keys from "w2016" to "2016"
all_waves <- setNames(waves, gsub("^w", "", names(waves)))

car_history <- build_car_history(all_waves)
print(car_history)
write_csv(car_history, "car_history.csv")


# Then run analysis with car history
results <- bind_rows(
  analyze_ev_ownership_data(car_history, 2019),
  analyze_ev_ownership_data(car_history, 2020),
  analyze_ev_ownership_data(car_history, 2021),
  analyze_ev_ownership_data(car_history, 2023),
  analyze_ev_ownership_data(car_history, 2025)
)

print(results)

# Print summary table

results %>%
  select(year, n_total, n_car_owners, n_ev_main, n_ev_secondary,
         n_ev_total, n_hybrid_gas, n_hybrid_diesel, ev_rate_car_owners) %>%
  mutate(
    ev_rate_car_owners = percent(ev_rate_car_owners, accuracy = 0.01)
  ) %>%
  kable(
    col.names = c("Year", "N", "Car Own.", "EV Main", "EV 2nd",
                  "EV Total", "Hybrid Gas", "Hybrid Dies.", "EV Rate"),
    align = "r",
    format.args = list(big.mark = ",")
  ) %>%
  print()



# Stacked bars with y-axis
p2 <- results %>%
  filter(year >= 2019) %>%
  select(year, n_ev_total, n_hybrid_gas, n_hybrid_diesel, n_car_owners) %>%
  mutate(
    n_hybrid_total = n_hybrid_gas + n_hybrid_diesel,
    total_electrified = n_ev_total + n_hybrid_total,
    total_pct = total_electrified / n_car_owners
  ) %>%
  select(year, n_ev_total, n_hybrid_total, n_car_owners, total_pct, total_electrified) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(n_ev_total, n_hybrid_total),
               names_to = "type",
               values_to = "count") %>%
  mutate(
    type = recode(type,
                  "n_ev_total" = "Pure Electric",
                  "n_hybrid_total" = "Hybrid"),
    type = factor(type, levels = c("Hybrid", "Pure Electric")),
    percentage = count / n_car_owners
  )

# Create separate totals dataset (one row per year)
totals_data <- p2 %>%
  group_by(year) %>%
  summarise(total_pct = first(total_pct), .groups = "drop")



#  Simple line comparison EV vs Hybrid
p3 <- results %>%
  filter(year >= 2019) %>%
  mutate(
    n_hybrid_total = n_hybrid_gas + n_hybrid_diesel
  ) %>%
  select(year, n_ev_total, n_hybrid_total) %>%
  pivot_longer(-year, names_to = "type", values_to = "count") %>%
  mutate(
    type = recode(type,
                  "n_ev_total" = "Pure Electric",
                  "n_hybrid_total" = "Hybrid")
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = year, y = count, color = type, group = type)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = count),
            vjust = -1, size = 3, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Pure Electric" = "#1B5E20", "Hybrid" = "#FF9800")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(results$year[results$year >= 2019])) +
  labs(
    title = "Pure Electric vs Hybrid Adoption Trends",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Number of Households",
    color = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

save_plot(p3, file.path(current_dir, "figures"),"ev_vs_hybrid_trend")

# Summary statistics
cat("EV GROWTH:\n")
results_clean <- results %>% filter(!is.na(ev_rate_car_owners))
cat("  2019:", percent(results_clean$ev_rate_car_owners[results_clean$year == 2019], accuracy = 0.01), "\n")
cat("  2025:", percent(results_clean$ev_rate_car_owners[results_clean$year == 2025], accuracy = 0.01), "\n")
cat("  Growth:",
    round(results_clean$ev_rate_car_owners[results_clean$year == 2025] /
          results_clean$ev_rate_car_owners[results_clean$year == 2019], 1), "x\n\n")

res_2025 <- results %>% filter(year == 2025)
cat("  Total EVs:", res_2025$n_ev_total, "\n")
cat("  Main car EVs:", res_2025$n_ev_main, "\n")
cat("  Secondary EVs:", res_2025$n_ev_secondary, "\n")
cat("  Total Hybrids:", res_2025$n_hybrid_gas + res_2025$n_hybrid_diesel, "\n")
```
